Building a Beginner-Friendly Social Media Platform with Django
📂 GitHub Repository:
https://github.com/tahaahmad-max/Codealpha_tasks/tree/main/Task_2_Social%20Media%20Platform
Building real-world applications is one of the best ways to improve programming skills. This time, I challenged myself to build a Social Media Platform using Django for the backend and HTML, CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript for the frontend.
The objective was to create a simple yet fully functional social media application that demonstrates core web development concepts while keeping the code clean, beginner-friendly, and easy to understand.
🛠️ Tech Stack

• Python

• Django

• HTML5

• CSS3

• Vanilla JavaScript

• SQLite
✨ Features
🏠 Home Feed
The homepage displays posts in chronological order with:

• User profile picture

• Username

• Caption

• Optional post image

• Date & time

• Like and Comment buttons
🔐 User Authentication
Using Django’s built-in authentication system, users can:

• Register

• Log in

• Log out
Only authenticated users can create posts, like posts, comment, and follow other users.
👤 User Profiles
Each user has a personalized profile containing:

• Profile picture

• Full name

• Username

• Bio

• Total posts

• Followers & Following counts

• Profile editing functionality

• User’s posts
✍️ Create & Manage Posts
Users can:

• Create text posts

• Upload images (optional)

• Edit their own posts

• Delete their own posts
💬 Comments
The platform allows users to:

• Add comments

• View discussions

• Delete their own comments
❤️ Like System
Users can :

• Like posts

• Unlike posts

• View the total number of likes on each post
👥 Follow System
Users can:

• Follow and unfollow other users

• View followers and following lists
🔍 Search Users
A simple search feature enables users to search by username and visit other users’ profiles.
⚙️ Django Admin Panel
The admin dashboard allows administrators to:

• Manage users

• Manage profiles

• Manage posts

• Manage comments

• Manage followers
🗄️ Database Design
The application is built around the following models:

• User (Django Authentication)

• UserProfile

• Post

• Comment

• Like

• Follow
These models helped me better understand database relationships, foreign keys, and many-to-many associations in Django.
📚 What I Learned
Building this project improved my understanding of:

• Django project architecture

• Models, Views, Templates, and URL routing

• User Authentication

• CRUD operations

• Media file uploads

• Database relationships

• Like and Follow functionality

• User search implementation

• Responsive frontend development

• Organizing scalable Django applications
This project also gave me practical experience implementing features commonly found in modern social media platforms.
🚀 Conclusion
Developing this Social Media Platform was a valuable learning experience that combined frontend development, backend logic, authentication, database management, and user interaction into one complete application.
There are many exciting enhancements that could be added in the future, such as real-time notifications, private messaging, hashtags, post sharing, email verification, and dark mode.
If you’re learning Django, I highly recommend building a project like this. It provides hands-on experience with real-world development and helps strengthen both backend and frontend skills.
Feel free to explore the project on GitHub, and I’d love to hear your feedback or suggestions. Happy coding!
